title: oenanthe aquatica
intro: Oenanthe aquatica, also known as water-parsnip or water celery, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its long, hollow stems and feathery leaves that resemble celery. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Oenanthe aquatica include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for gastrointestinal issues and respiratory problems. Oenanthe aquatica can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in some traditional Chinese medicine formulas, where it is valued for its ability to clear heat and detoxify the body.
